   786→
   787→== CLI
   788→
   789→[[jjdx_vvx]]
   790→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_program⟧
   791→{jjdx_vvx}::
   792→The Voce Viva executor.
   793→Local reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.
   794→
   795→[[jjdx_cli]]
   796→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_command_group⟧
   797→{jjdx_cli}::
   798→The Job Jockey command group within
   799→{jjdx_vvx}.
   800→Invoked as `vvx jjx`.
   801→All operations are subcommands of
   802→{jjdx_cli}.
   803→Inherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
   804→
   805→== Commit Message Architecture
   806→
   807→All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
   808→- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
   809→- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
   810→- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations
   811→
   812→== Operations
   813→
   814→Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
   815→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   816→{jjdgr_gallops}
   817→file access (read, transform, atomic write).
   818→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
   819→{jjda_file}.
   820→
   821→=== Write Operations
   822→
   823→[[jjdo_nominate]]
   824→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
   825→==== {jjdo_nominate}
   826→
   827→Create a new
   828→{jjdhr_heat}
   829→with empty
   830→{jjdpr_pace}
   831→structure.
   832→
   833→{jjds_arguments}
   834→
   835→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
   836→* {jjda_file}
   837→
   838→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   839→* {jjda_silks}
   840→
   841→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   842→* {jjda_created}
   843→
   844→{jjds_stdout} New
   845→{jjdt_firemark}
   846→
   847→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   848→
   849→{jjds_behavior}
   850→
   851→. If
   852→{jjdgr_gallops}
   853→file does not exist:
   854→.. Create parent directory `.claude/jjm/` if needed
   855→.. Initialize new
   856→{jjdgr_gallops}
   857→with
   858→{jjdgm_seed}
   859→`AA` and empty `heats`
   860→. Read
   861→{jjdgr_gallops}
   862→from file (or use newly created)
   863→. Read current
   864→{jjdgm_seed}
   865→as
   866→{jjdt_firemark}
   867→. Compute paddock path: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the 2 base64 chars
   868→. Create paddock file with template:
   869→+
   870→----
   871→# Paddock: {silks}
   872→
   873→## Context
   874→
   875→(Describe the initiative's background and goals)
   876→
   877→## References
   878→
   879→(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)
   880→----
   881→. Create new
   882→{jjdhr_heat}
   883→with:
   884→.. Key: `₣` + encoded
   885→{jjdt_firemark}
   886→.. {jjdhm_silks}:
   887→from argument
   888→.. {jjdhm_created}:
   889→from argument
   890→.. {jjdhm_status}:
   891→{jjdhe_current}
   892→.. {jjdhm_order}:
   893→empty array
   894→.. {jjdhm_seed}:
   895→`AAA` (index 0)
   896→.. {jjdhm_paddock}:
   897→computed paddock path
   898→.. `paces`: empty object
   899→. Increment
   900→{jjdgm_seed}
   901→(base64 arithmetic with carry)
   902→. Write transformed
   903→{jjdgr_gallops}
   904→to file (atomic)
   905→. Output new
   906→{jjdt_firemark}
   907→to stdout
   908→
   909→[[jjdo_slate]]
   910→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
   911→==== {jjdo_slate}
   912→
   913→Add a new
   914→{jjdpr_pace}
   915→to a
   916→{jjdhr_heat}.
   917→The initial
   918→{jjdkr_tack}
   919→is created with state
   920→{jjdpe_rough}.
   921→
   922→By default, the new
   923→{jjdpr_pace}
   924→is appended to the end of
   925→{jjdhm_order}.
   926→Use positioning flags to insert at a specific location.
   927→
   928→{jjds_arguments}
   929→
   930→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
   931→* {jjda_file}
   932→
   933→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   934→* {jjdt_firemark}
   935→(positional) — target
   936→{jjdhr_heat}
   937→
   938→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   939→* {jjda_silks}
   940→
   941→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   942→* stdin —
   943→{jjdkm_text}
   944→content for the initial
   945→{jjdkr_tack}
   946→
   947→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   948→* {jjda_before}
   949→— insert before specified
   950→{jjdt_coronet}
   951→
   952→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   953→* {jjda_after}
   954→— insert after specified
   955→{jjdt_coronet}
   956→
   957→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   958→* {jjda_first}
   959→— insert at beginning of
   960→{jjdhm_order}
   961→
   962→*Positioning:* Only one of
   963→{jjda_before},
   964→{jjda_after},
   965→or
   966→{jjda_first}
   967→may be specified.
   968→If none provided, append to end (default).
   969→Note:
   970→{jjdo_rail}
   971→reorders existing
   972→{jjdpr_pace_s};
   973→{jjdo_slate}
   974→positioning inserts a new
   975→{jjdpr_pace}
   976→at a specific location.
   977→
   978→{jjds_stdout} New
   979→{jjdt_coronet}
   980→
   981→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   982→
   983→{jjds_behavior}
   984→
   985→. Read
   986→{jjdgr_gallops}
   987→from file
   988→. Verify
   989→{jjdhr_heat}
   990→exists with given
   991→{jjdt_firemark}
   992→. If
   993→{jjda_before}
   994→or
   995→{jjda_after}
   996→provided, verify target
   997→{jjdt_coronet}
   998→exists in
   999→{jjdhr_heat}
  1000→. Read
  1001→{jjdhm_seed}
  1002→from target
  1003→{jjdhr_heat}
  1004→. Construct
  1005→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1006→`₢` + encoded
  1007→{jjdt_firemark}
  1008→(2 chars) +
  1009→{jjdhm_seed}
  1010→(3 chars)
  1011→. Create new
  1012→{jjdpr_pace}
  1013→with:
  1014→.. Key: constructed
  1015→{jjdt_coronet}
  1016→.. `tacks`: array with single
  1017→{jjdkr_tack}:
  1018→... {jjdkm_refined}:
  1019→current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
  1020→... {jjdkm_state}:
  1021→{jjdpe_rough}
  1022→... {jjdkm_text}:
  1023→from stdin
  1024→... {jjdkm_silks}:
  1025→from argument
  1026→... {jjdkm_commit}:
  1027→capture {jjdkm_commit}
  1028→. Insert
  1029→{jjdt_coronet}
  1030→into
  1031→{jjdhm_order}
  1032→at determined position:
  1033→.. If
  1034→{jjda_first}:
  1035→prepend to beginning
  1036→.. If
  1037→{jjda_before}:
  1038→insert immediately before target
  1039→{jjdt_coronet}
  1040→.. If
  1041→{jjda_after}:
  1042→insert immediately after target
  1043→{jjdt_coronet}
  1044→.. Otherwise: append to end
  1045→. Increment
  1046→{jjdhm_seed}
  1047→(base64 arithmetic with carry)
  1048→. Write transformed
  1049→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1050→to file (atomic)
  1051→. Output new
  1052→{jjdt_coronet}
  1053→to stdout
  1054→
  1055→[[jjdo_rail]]
  1056→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1057→==== {jjdo_rail}
  1058→
  1059→Reorder
  1060→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1061→within a
  1062→{jjdhr_heat}
  1063→by updating
  1064→{jjdhm_order}.
  1065→Supports two modes: *order mode* replaces the entire sequence; *move mode* relocates a single
  1066→{jjdpr_pace}.
  1067→
  1068→*Order mode* (full replacement):
  1069→----
  1070→vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> <CORONET>...
  1071→----
  1072→
  1073→*Move mode* (single relocation):
  1074→----
  1075→vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> --move <CORONET> [--before <TARGET> | --after <TARGET> | --first | --last]
  1076→----
  1077→
  1078→{jjds_arguments}
  1079→
  1080→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1081→* {jjda_file}
  1082→
  1083→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1084→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1085→(positional) — target
  1086→{jjdhr_heat}
  1087→
  1088→*Order mode arguments:*
  1089→
  1090→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1091→* order — array of
  1092→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1093→in desired sequence (space-separated positional arguments)
  1094→
  1095→*Move mode arguments:*
  1096→
  1097→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1098→* {jjda_move}
  1099→— {jjdt_coronet}
  1100→to relocate
  1101→
  1102→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1103→* Exactly one positioning flag:
  1104→{jjda_before},
  1105→{jjda_after},
  1106→{jjda_first},
  1107→or
  1108→{jjda_last}
  1109→
  1110→*Mode detection:* If
  1111→{jjda_move}
  1112→is present, move mode; otherwise order mode.
  1113→
  1114→{jjds_stdout} Updated
  1115→{jjdhm_order}
  1116→array, one
  1117→{jjdt_coronet}
  1118→per line, in new sequence.
  1119→
  1120→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1121→
  1122→{jjds_behavior}
  1123→
  1124→*Common steps:*
  1125→
  1126→. Read
  1127→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1128→from file
  1129→. Verify
  1130→{jjdhr_heat}
  1131→exists with given
  1132→{jjdt_firemark}
  1133→
  1134→*Order mode:*
  1135→
  1136→. Parse positional arguments into array of
  1137→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1138→. Validate order array:
  1139→.. Count must match current
  1140→{jjdhm_order}
  1141→length
  1142→.. No duplicate
  1143→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1144→.. All
  1145→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1146→must exist in
  1147→{jjdhr_heat_p}
  1148→`paces` object
  1149→.. All
  1150→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1151→must embed correct parent
  1152→{jjdt_firemark}
  1153→. Replace
  1154→{jjdhm_order}
  1155→with new order array
  1156→. Write transformed
  1157→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1158→to file (atomic)
  1159→. Output new
  1160→{jjdhm_order},
  1161→one
  1162→{jjdt_coronet}
  1163→per line
  1164→
  1165→*Move mode:*
  1166→
  1167→. Validate
  1168→{jjda_move}
  1169→{jjdt_coronet}
  1170→exists in
  1171→{jjdhr_heat}
  1172→. Validate exactly one positioning flag provided
  1173→. If
  1174→{jjda_before}
  1175→or
  1176→{jjda_after}:
  1177→validate target
  1178→{jjdt_coronet}
  1179→exists and differs from
  1180→{jjda_move}
  1181→{jjdt_coronet}
  1182→. Remove
  1183→{jjda_move}
  1184→{jjdt_coronet}
  1185→from current position in
  1186→{jjdhm_order}
  1187→. Insert at new position:
  1188→.. If
  1189→{jjda_first}:
  1190→prepend to beginning
  1191→.. If
  1192→{jjda_last}:
  1193→append to end
  1194→.. If
  1195→{jjda_before}:
  1196→insert immediately before target
  1197→{jjdt_coronet}
  1198→.. If
  1199→{jjda_after}:
  1200→insert immediately after target
  1201→{jjdt_coronet}
  1202→. Write transformed
  1203→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1204→to file (atomic)
  1205→. Output new
  1206→{jjdhm_order},
  1207→one
  1208→{jjdt_coronet}
  1209→per line
  1210→
  1211→*Validation errors:*
  1212→
  1213→[cols="2,3"]
  1214→|===
  1215→| Condition | Error
  1216→
  1217→| {jjda_move} with positional coronets
  1218→| "Cannot combine --move with positional coronets"
  1219→
  1220→| {jjda_move} {jjdt_coronet} not found
  1221→| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1222→
  1223→| No positioning flag provided
  1224→| "Move mode requires exactly one positioning flag"
  1225→
  1226→| Multiple positioning flags
  1227→| "Conflicting positioning flags: {flags}"
  1228→
  1229→| Target same as move
  1230→| "Cannot position pace relative to itself"
  1231→
  1232→| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found
  1233→| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1234→|===
  1235→
  1236→[[jjdo_draft]]
  1237→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1238→==== {jjdo_draft}
  1239→
  1240→Move a
  1241→{jjdpr_pace}
  1242→from one
  1243→{jjdhr_heat}
  1244→to another.
  1245→This is a primitive operation: mechanical transfer with no ceremony.
  1246→The
  1247→{jjdpr_pace}
  1248→receives a new
  1249→{jjdt_coronet}
  1250→in the destination
  1251→{jjdhr_heat};
  1252→all
  1253→{jjdkr_tack}
  1254→history is preserved.
  1255→
  1256→*Important:* "Drafted" is not a
  1257→{jjdpr_pace}
  1258→state.
  1259→{jjdpr_pace}
  1260→states remain:
  1261→{jjdpe_rough},
  1262→{jjdpe_bridled},
  1263→{jjdpe_complete},
  1264→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
  1265→Draft is an operation that moves
  1266→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1267→between
  1268→{jjdhr_heat_s};
  1269→state is preserved through the move.
  1270→
  1271→{jjds_arguments}
  1272→
  1273→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1274→* {jjda_file}
  1275→
  1276→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1277→* {jjdt_coronet}
  1278→(positional) —
  1279→{jjdpr_pace}
  1280→to move
  1281→
  1282→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1283→* {jjda_to}
  1284→— destination
  1285→{jjdt_firemark}
  1286→
  1287→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1288→* {jjda_before}
  1289→— insert before specified
  1290→{jjdt_coronet}
  1291→in destination
  1292→
  1293→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1294→* {jjda_after}
  1295→— insert after specified
  1296→{jjdt_coronet}
  1297→in destination
  1298→
  1299→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1300→* {jjda_first}
  1301→— insert at beginning of destination
  1302→{jjdhm_order}
  1303→
  1304→*Positioning:* Only one of
  1305→{jjda_before},
  1306→{jjda_after},
  1307→or
  1308→{jjda_first}
  1309→may be specified.
  1310→If none provided, append to end of destination
  1311→{jjdhm_order}
  1312→(default).
  1313→
  1314→{jjds_stdout} New
  1315→{jjdt_coronet}
  1316→in destination
  1317→{jjdhr_heat}
  1318→
  1319→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1320→
  1321→{jjds_behavior}
  1322→
  1323→. Read
  1324→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1325→from file
  1326→. Extract source
  1327→{jjdt_firemark}
  1328→from
  1329→{jjdt_coronet}
  1330→(first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1331→. Verify source
  1332→{jjdhr_heat}
  1333→exists
  1334→. Verify
  1335→{jjdpr_pace}
  1336→exists in source
  1337→{jjdhr_heat}
  1338→. Verify destination
  1339→{jjdhr_heat}
  1340→exists with given
  1341→{jjdt_firemark}
  1342→. If
  1343→{jjda_before}
  1344→or
  1345→{jjda_after}
  1346→provided, verify target
  1347→{jjdt_coronet}
  1348→exists in destination
  1349→{jjdhr_heat}
  1350→. Read destination
  1351→{jjdhm_seed}
  1352→. Construct new
  1353→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1354→`₢` + destination
  1355→{jjdt_firemark}
  1356→(2 chars) + destination
  1357→{jjdhm_seed}
  1358→(3 chars)
  1359→. Copy
  1360→{jjdpr_pace}
  1361→data to destination with new
  1362→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1363→.. Prepend new
  1364→{jjdkr_tack}
  1365→recording the draft:
  1366→... {jjdkm_refined}:
  1367→current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
  1368→... {jjdkm_state}:
  1369→inherited from previous `tacks[0].state`
  1370→... {jjdkm_text}:
  1371→`Drafted from {old_coronet} in {source_firemark}.\n\n` + previous `tacks[0].text`
  1372→... {jjdkm_silks}:
  1373→inherited from previous `tacks[0].silks`
  1374→... {jjdkm_commit}:
  1375→capture {jjdkm_commit}
  1376→.. Preserve remaining
  1377→{jjdkr_tack}
  1378→history
  1379→. Insert new
  1380→{jjdt_coronet}
  1381→into destination
  1382→{jjdhm_order}
  1383→at determined position
  1384→. Increment destination
  1385→{jjdhm_seed}
  1386→. Remove
  1387→{jjdpr_pace}
  1388→from source
  1389→{jjdhr_heat}:
  1390→.. Remove from source `paces` object
  1391→.. Remove from source
  1392→{jjdhm_order}
  1393→. Write transformed
  1394→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1395→to file (atomic)
  1396→. Output new
  1397→{jjdt_coronet}
  1398→to stdout
  1399→
  1400→*Validation errors:*
  1401→
  1402→[cols="2,3"]
  1403→|===
  1404→| Condition | Error
  1405→
  1406→| Source and destination same
  1407→| "Cannot draft pace to same heat"
  1408→
  1409→| Source {jjdpr_pace} not found
  1410→| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1411→
  1412→| Destination {jjdhr_heat} not found
  1413→| "Heat {firemark} not found"
  1414→
  1415→| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found in destination
  1416→| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1417→|===
  1418→
  1419→[[jjdo_tally]]
  1420→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1421→==== {jjdo_tally}
  1422→
  1423→Add a new
  1424→{jjdkr_tack}
  1425→to a
  1426→{jjdpr_pace}.
  1427→This is the unified operation for all
  1428→{jjdkr_tack}
  1429→creation: state transitions, plan refinements, or both.
  1430→
  1431→{jjds_arguments}
  1432→
  1433→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1434→* {jjda_file}
  1435→
  1436→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1437→* {jjdt_coronet}
  1438→(positional)
  1439→
  1440→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1441→* {jjda_state}
  1442→— if absent, inherits from previous
  1443→{jjdkr_tack}
  1444→
  1445→// ⟦axd_conditional⟧
  1446→* {jjda_direction}
  1447→— required if
  1448→{jjda_state}
  1449→is
  1450→{jjdpe_bridled};
  1451→forbidden if
  1452→{jjda_state}
  1453→is provided and not
  1454→{jjdpe_bridled};
  1455→ignored if
  1456→{jjda_state}
  1457→is absent (inherits with state)
  1458→
  1459→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1460→* stdin —
  1461→{jjdkm_text}
  1462→content; if absent, inherits from previous
  1463→{jjdkr_tack}
  1464→
  1465→{jjds_stdout} None
  1466→
  1467→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1468→
  1469→{jjds_behavior}
  1470→
  1471→. Read
  1472→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1473→from file
  1474→. Extract parent
  1475→{jjdt_firemark}
  1476→from
  1477→{jjdt_coronet}
  1478→(first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1479→. Verify
  1480→{jjdhr_heat}
  1481→exists
  1482→. Verify
  1483→{jjdpr_pace}
  1484→exists with given
  1485→{jjdt_coronet}
  1486→. Read current
  1487→{jjdkr_tack}
  1488→from `tacks[0]`
  1489→. Determine new state:
  1490→.. If
  1491→{jjda_state}
  1492→provided: use that value
  1493→.. Otherwise: inherit from previous
  1494→{jjdkr_tack}
  1495→. Determine new direction:
  1496→.. If
  1497→{jjda_state}
  1498→is
  1499→{jjdpe_bridled}:
  1500→use
  1501→{jjda_direction}
  1502→argument (required)
  1503→.. If
  1504→{jjda_state}
  1505→is absent and previous state is
  1506→{jjdpe_bridled}:
  1507→inherit from previous
  1508→{jjdkr_tack}
  1509→.. Otherwise: omit
  1510→{jjdkm_direction}
  1511→. Determine new text:
  1512→.. If stdin provided: use stdin content
  1513→.. Otherwise: inherit from previous
  1514→{jjdkr_tack}
  1515→. Create new
  1516→{jjdkr_tack}
  1517→with:
  1518→.. {jjdkm_refined}:
  1519→current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
  1520→.. {jjdkm_state}:
  1521→determined above
  1522→.. {jjdkm_text}:
  1523→determined above
  1524→.. {jjdkm_silks}:
  1525→inherited from previous `tacks[0].silks`
  1526→.. {jjdkm_commit}:
  1527→capture {jjdkm_commit}
  1528→.. {jjdkm_direction}:
  1529→determined above (if applicable)
  1530→. Prepend new
  1531→{jjdkr_tack}
  1532→to `tacks` array (position 0)
  1533→. Write transformed
  1534→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1535→to file (atomic)
  1536→
  1537→[[jjdo_retire]]
  1538→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1539→==== {jjdo_retire}
  1540→
  1541→Archive a
  1542→{jjdhr_heat}
  1543→by writing a trophy file, removing the heat from
  1544→{jjdgr_gallops},
  1545→deleting the paddock file, and committing.
  1546→This is a destructive operation that permanently removes the heat from active tracking.
  1547→
  1548→{jjds_arguments}
  1549→
  1550→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1551→* {jjda_file}
  1552→
  1553→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1554→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1555→(positional) — target
  1556→{jjdhr_heat}
  1557→
  1558→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1559→* `--execute` — perform the retire operation.
  1560→Without this flag, outputs trophy markdown to stdout as a dry-run preview.
  1561→
  1562→{jjds_stdout}
  1563→Without `--execute`: trophy markdown content (dry-run preview).
  1564→With `--execute`: path to the written trophy file.
  1565→
  1566→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1567→
  1568→{jjds_behavior}
  1569→
  1570→Without `--execute` (dry-run):
  1571→
  1572→. Read
  1573→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1574→from file
  1575→. Verify
  1576→{jjdhr_heat}
  1577→exists with given
  1578→{jjdt_firemark}
  1579→. Read paddock file contents from
  1580→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1581→. Call
  1582→{jjdo_rein}
  1583→internally to get steeplechase entries
  1584→. Build trophy markdown (see format below)
  1585→. Output trophy markdown to stdout
  1586→
  1587→With `--execute`:
  1588→
  1589→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1590→. Read
  1591→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1592→from file
  1593→. Verify
  1594→{jjdhr_heat}
  1595→exists with given
  1596→{jjdt_firemark}
  1597→. Extract
  1598→{jjdhr_heat}
  1599→metadata:
  1600→{jjdhm_silks},
  1601→{jjdhm_created}
  1602→. Read paddock file contents from
  1603→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1604→. Call
  1605→{jjdo_rein}
  1606→internally to get steeplechase entries
  1607→. Build trophy markdown:
  1608→+
  1609→----
  1610→# Heat Trophy: {silks}
  1611→
  1612→**Firemark:** {firemark}
  1613→**Created:** {created}
  1614→**Retired:** {today}
  1615→**Status:** retired
  1616→
  1617→## Paddock
  1618→
  1619→{full paddock content}
  1620→
  1621→## Paces
  1622→
  1623→{for each pace in order:}
  1624→
  1625→### {pace_silks} ({coronet}) [{final_state}]
  1626→
  1627→{for each tack, newest first:}
  1628→**[{ts}] {state}**
  1629→
  1630→{tack text}
  1631→
  1632→{if tack has direction:}
  1633→*Direction:* {direction}
  1634→
  1635→## Steeplechase
  1636→
  1637→{for each steeplechase entry from rein, newest first:}
  1638→
  1639→### {date} - {pace_silks or "Heat"} - {marker or "commit"}
  1640→
  1641→{subject/description}
  1642→
  1643→----
  1644→. Compute trophy filename: `.claude/jjm/retired/jjh_b{created}-r{today}-{silks}.md`
  1645→where `{today}` is current date in YYMMDD format
  1646→. Create `.claude/jjm/retired/` directory if it does not exist
  1647→. Write trophy markdown to trophy file
  1648→. Remove
  1649→{jjdhr_heat}
  1650→entry from
  1651→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1652→`heats` object
  1653→. Write transformed
  1654→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1655→to file (atomic)
  1656→. Delete paddock file at
  1657→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1658→. Stage exactly three paths: trophy file, gallops.json, paddock file (as deletion)
  1659→. Run size guard with 200KB limit (trophy files can be large for big heats)
  1660→. Commit with message per steeplechase conventions (format TBD by unify-commit-format)
  1661→. Release lock
  1662→. Output trophy file path to stdout
  1663→
  1664→=== Read Operations
  1665→
  1666→[[jjdo_validate]]
  1667→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1668→==== {jjdo_validate}
  1669→
  1670→Check that
  1671→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1672→JSON conforms to schema.
  1673→
  1674→{jjds_arguments}
  1675→
  1676→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1677→* {jjda_file}
  1678→
  1679→{jjds_stdout} None
  1680→
  1681→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1682→
  1683→*Validation Rules:*
  1684→
  1685→. Root must be JSON object with
  1686→{jjdgm_seed}
  1687→(2 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`)
  1688→. Root must have `heats` object
  1689→. Each
  1690→{jjdhr_heat}
  1691→key must match `₣[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`
  1692→. Each
  1693→{jjdhr_heat}
  1694→must have:
  1695→{jjdhm_silks}
  1696→(non-empty kebab-case),
  1697→{jjdhm_created}
  1698→(YYMMDD),
  1699→{jjdhm_status}
  1700→({jjdhe_current}
  1701→or
  1702→{jjdhe_retired}),
  1703→{jjdhm_order}
  1704→(array),
  1705→{jjdhm_seed}
  1706→(3 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{3}`),
  1707→`paces` (object)
  1708→. {jjdhm_order}
  1709→array and `paces` object must have identical key sets
  1710→. Each
  1711→{jjdpr_pace}
  1712→key must match `₢[A-Za-z0-9_-]{5}` and embed parent
  1713→{jjdhr_heat}
  1714→identity (first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1715→. Each
  1716→{jjdpr_pace}
  1717→must have:
  1718→`tacks` (non-empty array)
  1719→. Each
  1720→{jjdkr_tack}
  1721→must have:
  1722→{jjdkm_refined}
  1723→(YYMMDD-HHMM),
  1724→{jjdkm_state}
  1725→({jjdpe_rough},
  1726→{jjdpe_bridled},
  1727→{jjdpe_complete},
  1728→or
  1729→{jjdpe_abandoned}),
  1730→{jjdkm_text}
  1731→(non-empty string),
  1732→{jjdkm_silks}
  1733→(non-empty kebab-case),
  1734→{jjdkm_commit}
  1735→(valid {jjdkm_commit} format)
  1736→. If
  1737→{jjdkm_state}
  1738→is
  1739→{jjdpe_bridled},
  1740→{jjdkm_direction}
  1741→must be present (non-empty string); otherwise
  1742→{jjdkm_direction}
  1743→must be absent
  1744→
  1745→[[jjdo_muster]]
  1746→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1747→==== {jjdo_muster}
  1748→
  1749→List all
  1750→{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1751→with summary information.
  1752→
  1753→{jjds_arguments}
  1754→
  1755→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1756→* {jjda_file}
  1757→
  1758→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1759→* {jjda_status}
  1760→— filter to show only
  1761→{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1762→with this status; if absent, show all
  1763→
  1764→{jjds_stdout} Tab-separated values, one line per
  1765→{jjdhr_heat}:
  1766→`{jjdt_firemark}<TAB>{jjdt_silks}<TAB>{jjdhm_status}<TAB><pace_count>`
  1767→
  1768→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1769→
  1770→{jjds_behavior}
  1771→
  1772→. Read
  1773→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1774→from file
  1775→. Iterate over `heats` object entries
  1776→. If
  1777→{jjda_status}
  1778→provided, skip
  1779→{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1780→not matching filter
  1781→. For each included
  1782→{jjdhr_heat},
  1783→output TSV line:
  1784→{jjdt_firemark},
  1785→{jjdhm_silks},
  1786→{jjdhm_status},
  1787→count of
  1788→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1789→in `paces` object
  1790→. Output lines in arbitrary order (JSON object iteration order)
  1791→
  1792→[[jjdo_saddle]]
  1793→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1794→==== {jjdo_saddle}
  1795→
  1796→Return context needed to saddle up on a
  1797→{jjdhr_heat}:
  1798→the current
  1799→{jjdpr_pace}
  1800→(first in
  1801→{jjdhm_order}
  1802→whose state is
  1803→{jjdpe_rough}
  1804→or
  1805→{jjdpe_bridled})
  1806→and its
  1807→{jjdkr_tack}
  1808→details.
  1809→
  1810→{jjds_arguments}
  1811→
  1812→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1813→* {jjda_file}
  1814→
  1815→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1816→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1817→(positional) — target
  1818→{jjdhr_heat}
  1819→
  1820→{jjds_stdout} JSON object with structure:
  1821→[source,json]
  1822→----
  1823→{
  1824→  "heat_silks": "...",
  1825→  "paddock_file": ".claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md",
  1826→  "paddock_content": "...",
  1827→  "pace_coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  1828→  "pace_silks": "...",
  1829→  "pace_state": "rough|bridled",
  1830→  "spec": "...",
  1831→  "direction": "...",
  1832→  "recent_work": [
  1833→    {"timestamp": "...", "commit": "...", "coronet": "...", "action": "...", "subject": "..."}
  1834→  ]
  1835→}
  1836→----
  1837→Fields `pace_coronet` through `direction` are absent if no actionable pace exists.
  1838→Field `pace_silks` is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
  1839→Field `direction` is present only when `pace_state` is
  1840→{jjdpe_bridled}.
  1841→Field `paddock_content` contains the full text of the paddock file.
  1842→Field `recent_work` contains the last 10 steeplechase entries for the heat (from
  1843→{jjdo_rein}).
  1844→
  1845→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1846→
  1847→{jjds_behavior}
  1848→
  1849→. Read
  1850→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1851→from file
  1852→. Verify
  1853→{jjdhr_heat}
  1854→exists with given
  1855→{jjdt_firemark}
  1856→. Output `heat_silks` from
  1857→{jjdhr_heat}
  1858→. Output `paddock_file` from
  1859→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1860→. Read paddock file contents, output as `paddock_content`
  1861→. Walk
  1862→{jjdhm_order}
  1863→array in sequence
  1864→. For each
  1865→{jjdt_coronet},
  1866→check `paces[coronet].tacks[0].state`
  1867→. If state is
  1868→{jjdpe_rough}
  1869→or
  1870→{jjdpe_bridled}:
  1871→.. Output `pace_coronet`,
  1872→`pace_silks` (from `tacks[0].silks`),
  1873→`pace_state`
  1874→.. Output `spec` from `tacks[0].text`
  1875→.. If state is
  1876→{jjdpe_bridled},
  1877→output `direction` from `tacks[0].direction`
  1878→.. Stop walking (first match wins)
  1879→. If no actionable pace found, output only `heat_silks`, `paddock_file`, `paddock_content`, and `recent_work`
  1880→. Get last 10 steeplechase entries for the heat via
  1881→{jjdo_rein}
  1882→logic, output as `recent_work` array
  1883→
  1884→[[jjdo_parade]]
  1885→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1886→==== {jjdo_parade}
  1887→
  1888→Display
  1889→{jjdhr_heat}
  1890→status in human-readable text format.
  1891→Output varies by format mode.
  1892→
  1893→{jjds_arguments}
  1894→
  1895→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1896→* {jjda_file}
  1897→
  1898→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1899→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1900→(positional) — target
  1901→{jjdhr_heat}
  1902→
  1903→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1904→* `--format <mode>` — output format mode (default: `full`)
  1905→
  1906→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1907→* `--pace <coronet>` — target
  1908→{jjdt_coronet}
  1909→(accepts with or without `₢` prefix; falls back to
  1910→{jjdts_silks}
  1911→match if coronet parse fails or pace not found by coronet).
  1912→Required with `--format detail`
  1913→
  1914→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1915→* `--remaining` — show only remaining paces (exclude
  1916→{jjdpe_complete}
  1917→and
  1918→{jjdpe_abandoned})
  1919→
  1920→*Format Modes*
  1921→
  1922→[cols="1,2,3"]
  1923→|===
  1924→| Mode | Purpose | Output
  1925→
  1926→| `overview`
  1927→| Quick status
  1928→| One line per pace: `[state] silks (₢coronet)`
  1929→
  1930→| `order`
  1931→| Dependency check
  1932→| Numbered: `N. [state] silks (₢coronet)`
  1933→
  1934→| `detail`
  1935→| Inspect one pace
  1936→| Full tack text for specified pace (requires `--pace`)
  1937→
  1938→| `full`
  1939→| Planning context
  1940→| Paddock content + all paces with tack text
  1941→|===
  1942→
  1943→{jjds_stdout} Human-readable text (not JSON).
  1944→
  1945→*Example output — overview:*
  1946→----
  1947→[abandoned] vvw-workbench-tabtarget (₢AAAAC)
  1948→[complete] install-arch-decision (₢AAAAI)
  1949→[rough] vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1950→----
  1951→
  1952→*Example output — order:*
  1953→----
  1954→1. [abandoned] vvw-workbench-tabtarget (₢AAAAC)
  1955→2. [complete] install-arch-decision (₢AAAAI)
  1956→3. [rough] vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1957→----
  1958→
  1959→*Example output — detail:*
  1960→----
  1961→Pace: vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1962→State: rough
  1963→Heat: ₣AA
  1964→
  1965→[full tack text...]
  1966→----
  1967→
  1968→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1969→
  1970→{jjds_behavior}
  1971→
  1972→. Validate arguments (if `--format detail`, require `--pace`)
  1973→. Read
  1974→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1975→from file
  1976→. Verify
  1977→{jjdhr_heat}
  1978→exists with given
  1979→{jjdt_firemark}
  1980→. Match on format mode:
  1981→.. *overview*: For each pace in
  1982→{jjdhm_order},
  1983→print `[state] silks (coronet)` where silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`
  1984→.. *order*: Same as overview with line numbers
  1985→.. *detail*: Find specified pace, print header and full tack text
  1986→.. *full*: Print heat header, paddock content, then each pace with tack text
  1987→
  1988→=== Steeplechase Operations
  1989→
  1990→The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  1991→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1992→JSON.
  1993→Commit messages follow patterns that
  1994→{jjdo_rein}
  1995→can parse to reconstruct session history.
  1996→
  1997→==== Commit Message Patterns
  1998→
  1999→All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:IDENTITY[:ACTION]: message`
  2000→
  2001→* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
  2002→* `BRAND` — repository identifier baked into arcanum at install time (e.g., "RBM")
  2003→* `HALLMARK` — Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  2004→* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
  2005→* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)
  2006→
  2007→*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  2008→----
  2009→jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:n: commit message
  2010→
  2011→Co-Authored-By: ...
  2012→----
  2013→
  2014→Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  2015→
  2016→*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  2017→----
  2018→jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:A: description
  2019→
  2020→Co-Authored-By: ...
  2021→----
  2022→
  2023→Pace-level action codes:
  2024→
  2025→* `n` — Notch: standard commit
  2026→* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  2027→* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
  2028→* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  2029→* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  2030→
  2031→*Heat-level operations*:
  2032→----
  2033→jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:N: heat-silks
  2034→jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:S: pace-silks
  2035→jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:r: reordered
  2036→jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:T: pace-silks
  2037→jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
  2038→jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:R: heat-silks
  2039→----
  2040→
  2041→Heat-level action codes:
  2042→
  2043→* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
  2044→* `S` — Slate: add new pace
  2045→* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  2046→* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
  2047→* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  2048→* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
  2049→
  2050→[[jjdo_rein]]
  2051→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2052→==== {jjdo_rein}
  2053→
  2054→Parse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
  2055→{jjdhr_heat}.
  2056→
  2057→{jjds_arguments}
  2058→
  2059→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2060→* {jjdt_firemark}
  2061→(positional) — target
  2062→{jjdhr_heat}
  2063→
  2064→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2065→* `--limit` — maximum entries to return (default: 50)
  2066→
  2067→{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
  2068→[source,json]
  2069→----
  2070→[
  2071→  {
  2072→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2073→    "commit": "abc123ef",
  2074→    "hallmark": "1009",
  2075→    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  2076→    "action": "n",
  2077→    "subject": "commit message"
  2078→  },
  2079→  {
  2080→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2081→    "commit": "def456ab",
  2082→    "hallmark": "1009-abc1234",
  2083→    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  2084→    "action": "A",
  2085→    "subject": "description"
  2086→  },
  2087→  {
  2088→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2089→    "hallmark": "1009",
  2090→    "action": "N",
  2091→    "subject": "heat-silks"
  2092→  }
  2093→]
  2094→----
  2095→Field `hallmark` is present for new-format commits, absent for old-format commits.
  2096→Field `coronet` is present for pace-level entries, absent for heat-level operations.
  2097→Field `action` is present for all commits, including standard notch commits (`n`).
  2098→Entries ordered newest first.
  2099→
  2100→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2101→
  2102→{jjds_behavior}
  2103→
  2104→. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:[^:]+:(₣XX|₢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  2105→  (matches any brand; filtering is by identity, not brand)
  2106→. Parse each line: extract timestamp, identity, action (if present), and message
  2107→. For pace-level (₢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
  2108→. For heat-level (₣FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
  2109→. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
  2110→. Output JSON
  2111→
  2112→[[jjdo_notch]]
  2113→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2114→==== {jjdo_notch}
  2115→
  2116→Create a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.
  2117→
  2118→{jjds_arguments}
  2119→
  2120→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2121→* {jjdt_coronet}
  2122→(positional) — active pace identity (embeds parent heat)
  2123→
  2124→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2125→* `--size-limit` — size limit in bytes for the commit guard (default: 50KB).
  2126→Use when the pace spec justifies legitimate large commits.
  2127→
  2128→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2129→
  2130→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2131→
  2132→{jjds_behavior}
  2133→
  2134→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2135→. Stage changes (`git add -u`)
  2136→. Run size guard check (uses `--size-limit` if provided, otherwise 50KB default)
  2137→. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
  2138→. Format full message: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2139→. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  2140→. Release lock
  2141→. Output commit hash
  2142→
  2143→[[jjdo_chalk]]
  2144→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2145→==== {jjdo_chalk}
  2146→
  2147→Create an empty commit marking a steeplechase event.
  2148→
  2149→{jjds_arguments}
  2150→
  2151→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2152→* identity (positional) — {jjdt_coronet} for pace-level, {jjdt_firemark} for heat-level discussion
  2153→
  2154→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2155→* `--marker` — marker type: `A`, `W`, `F`, or `d` (single letter codes)
  2156→
  2157→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2158→* `--description` — marker description text
  2159→
  2160→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2161→
  2162→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2163→
  2164→{jjds_behavior}
  2165→
  2166→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2167→. Detect identity type by length (5 chars = coronet, 2 chars = firemark)
  2168→. Validate: `A`/`W`/`F` require coronet; `d` accepts either
  2169→. Format message:
  2170→.. If coronet: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:X: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2171→.. If firemark (discussion only): `jjb:BRAND:₣XX:d: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2172→. Run `git commit --allow-empty -m "..."`
  2173→. Release lock
  2174→. Output commit hash
  2175→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

= Job Jockey Gallops Data Model
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// jjdt_:  Types (scalar value types with validation rules)
// jjdgr_: Gallops record
// jjdgm_: Gallops members
// jjdhr_: Heat record
// jjdhm_: Heat members
// jjdhe_: Heat enum values (status)
// jjdpr_: Pace record
// jjdpm_: Pace members
// jjdkr_: Tack record
// jjdkm_: Tack members
// jjdpe_: Tack state enum values
// jjdo_:  Operations
// jjds_:  Section headers (operation documentation structure)
// jjdz_:  Serialization concerns
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//

// Types
:jjdt_coronet:    <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet>>
:jjdt_coronet_p:  <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet's>>
:jjdt_coronet_s:  <<jjdt_coronet,Coronets>>
:jjdt_firemark:   <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark>>
:jjdt_firemark_p: <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark's>>
:jjdt_firemark_s: <<jjdt_firemark,Firemarks>>
:jjdt_silks:      <<jjdt_silks,Silks>>

// Gallops record and members
:jjdgm_seed:        <<jjdgm_seed,next_heat_seed>>
:jjdgr_gallops:     <<jjdgr_gallops,Gallops>>
:jjdgr_gallops_p:   <<jjdgr_gallops,Gallops'>>

// Heat record and members
:jjdhe_current:   <<jjdhe_current,current>>
:jjdhe_retired:   <<jjdhe_retired,retired>>
:jjdhm_created:   <<jjdhm_created,creation_time>>
:jjdhm_firemark:  <<jjdhm_firemark,firemark>>
:jjdhm_order:     <<jjdhm_order,order>>
:jjdhm_paddock:   <<jjdhm_paddock,paddock_file>>
:jjdhm_seed:      <<jjdhm_seed,next_pace_seed>>
:jjdhm_silks:     <<jjdhm_silks,silks>>
:jjdhm_status:    <<jjdhm_status,status>>
:jjdhr_heat:      <<jjdhr_heat,Heat>>
:jjdhr_heat_p:    <<jjdhr_heat,Heat's>>
:jjdhr_heat_s:    <<jjdhr_heat,Heats>>

// Pace record and members
:jjdpm_coronet:   <<jjdpm_coronet,coronet>>
:jjdpr_pace:      <<jjdpr_pace,Pace>>
:jjdpr_pace_p:    <<jjdpr_pace,Pace's>>
:jjdpr_pace_s:    <<jjdpr_pace,Paces>>

// Pace/Tack state enum values (used in Tack)
:jjdpe_abandoned: <<jjdpe_abandoned,abandoned>>
:jjdpe_complete:  <<jjdpe_complete,complete>>
:jjdpe_bridled:   <<jjdpe_bridled,bridled>>
:jjdpe_rough:     <<jjdpe_rough,rough>>

// Tack record and members
:jjdkm_commit:    <<jjdkm_commit,commit>>
:jjdkm_direction: <<jjdkm_direction,direction>>
:jjdkm_refined:   <<jjdkm_refined,ts>>
:jjdkm_silks:     <<jjdkm_silks,silks>>
:jjdkm_state:     <<jjdkm_state,state>>
:jjdkm_text:      <<jjdkm_text,text>>
:jjdkr_tack:      <<jjdkr_tack,Tack>>
:jjdkr_tack_p:    <<jjdkr_tack,Tack's>>
:jjdkr_tack_s:    <<jjdkr_tack,Tacks>>

// Arguments
:jjda_after:          <<jjda_after,--after>>
:jjda_before:         <<jjda_before,--before>>
:jjda_created:        <<jjda_created,--created>>
:jjda_direction:      <<jjda_direction,--direction>>
:jjda_file:           <<jjda_file,--file>>
:jjda_first:          <<jjda_first,--first>>
:jjda_last:           <<jjda_last,--last>>
:jjda_move:           <<jjda_move,--move>>
:jjda_pace:           <<jjda_pace,--pace>>
:jjda_silks:          <<jjda_silks,--silks>>
:jjda_state:          <<jjda_state,--state>>
:jjda_status:         <<jjda_status,--status>>
:jjda_to:             <<jjda_to,--to>>

// CLI executor
:jjdx_vvx:   <<jjdx_vvx,vvx>>
:jjdx_cli:   <<jjdx_cli,jjx>>

// Operations
:jjdo_canter:         <<jjdo_canter,jjx_canter>>
:jjdo_chalk:          <<jjdo_chalk,jjx_chalk>>
:jjdo_draft:          <<jjdo_draft,jjx_draft>>
:jjdo_muster:         <<jjdo_muster,jjx_muster>>
:jjdo_nominate:       <<jjdo_nominate,jjx_nominate>>
:jjdo_notch:          <<jjdo_notch,jjx_notch>>
:jjdo_rail:           <<jjdo_rail,jjx_rail>>
:jjdo_rein:           <<jjdo_rein,jjx_rein>>
:jjdo_retire:         <<jjdo_retire,jjx_retire>>
:jjdo_saddle:         <<jjdo_saddle,jjx_saddle>>
:jjdo_slate:          <<jjdo_slate,jjx_slate>>
:jjdo_tally:          <<jjdo_tally,jjx_tally>>
:jjdo_validate:       <<jjdo_validate,jjx_validate>>

// Section headers
:jjds_arguments:        <<jjds_arguments,Arguments:>>
:jjds_behavior:         <<jjds_behavior,Behavior:>>
:jjds_exit_enumerated:  <<jjds_exit_enumerated,Exit Status:>>
:jjds_exit_uniform:     <<jjds_exit_uniform,Exit Status:>>
:jjds_stdout:           <<jjds_stdout,Stdout:>>

// Serialization
:jjdz_charset:  <<jjdz_charset,Charset>>
:jjdz_encoding: <<jjdz_encoding,Identity Encoding>>

// end::mapping-section[]

== Overview

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
It tracks
{jjdhr_heat_s}
(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
{jjdpr_pace_s}
(discrete actions).
Each
{jjdpr_pace}
maintains a history of
{jjdkr_tack_s}
(plan refinements).

== Design Principles

=== Exclusive JSON Ownership

All access to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.

The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
The internet is full of bad bash.
Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
The compiler is tireless; humans are not.

=== Git as Journal

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.

The
{jjdkr_tack}
history within each
{jjdpr_pace}
exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
Git has the full history, but
{jjdkr_tack_s}
let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.

=== Diff-Friendly Mutations

New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
prepend to position 0 rather than append.
This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.

The
{jjdhm_order}
array contains full
{jjdt_coronet_s}.
Each
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(redundant within a single
{jjdhr_heat}).
This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.

=== Crash-Safe Architecture

Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.

The pattern:
[source,bash]
----
lock_acquire
vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
lock_release
----

This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.

=== Deterministic Serialization

Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
This ensures that reordering paces via
{jjdo_rail}
produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.

=== Identity Encoding

{jjdt_firemark}
(`₣` prefix) and
{jjdt_coronet}
(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.

=== Horse Racing Vocabulary

Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.

* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)

== Types

[[jjdt_firemark]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_firemark}::
The identity of a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.

[[jjdt_coronet]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_coronet}::
The identity of a
{jjdpr_pace}.
Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
{jjdhr_heat_p}
encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
+
*Reassignment:* When a
{jjdpr_pace}
is moved between
{jjdhr_heat_s}
via
{jjdo_draft},
the old
{jjdt_coronet}
becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
{jjdhr_heat_p}
{jjdhm_seed}.
The new
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds the destination
{jjdt_firemark}.
{jjdkr_tack}
history is preserved, with a note recording the original
{jjdt_coronet}.

[[jjdt_silks]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_silks}::
A kebab-case display name.
Used for human-readable identification of
{jjdhr_heat_s}
and
{jjdpr_pace_s}.
Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`

== Records

=== {jjdgr_gallops}

[[jjdgr_gallops]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdgr_gallops}::
The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.

==== Members

* [[jjdgm_seed]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdgm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_firemark}
to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_nominate}.

=== {jjdhr_heat}

[[jjdhr_heat]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdhr_heat}::
A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
Contains zero or more
{jjdpr_pace_s}
representing discrete actions.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_firemark}.

==== Members

* [[jjdhm_firemark]]

=== {jjdkr_tack}

[[jjdkr_tack]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdkr_tack}::
A snapshot of
{jjdpr_pace}
state and plan at a point in time.
{jjdkr_tack_s}
form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
Position 0 is the most recent; older
{jjdkr_tack_s}
follow.
New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
The current
{jjdpr_pace}

`tacks[0].state`.

* [[jjdkm_direction]]

==== State Values

[[jjdpe_rough]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdpe_rough}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
needs clarification before work can proceed.
When context is assembled for a
{jjdpe_rough}
pace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.

[[jjdpe_bridled]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdpe_bridled}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
is ready for autonomous execution.
The

When context is assembled for a
{jjdpe_bridled}

[[jjdpe_complete]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdpe_complete}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}

[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdpe_abandoned}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
was stopped without completion.

== Serialization

[[jjdz_charset]]
{jjdz_charset}::
The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.

[[jjdz_encoding]]
{jjdz_encoding}::
The algorithm for encoding
{jjdt_firemark}
and
{jjdt_coronet}
values.
+
*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
+
. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
+
*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
+
. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
+
*Input flexibility:*
+
CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

== CLI

[[jjdx_vvx]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_program⟧
{jjdx_vvx}::
The Voce Viva executor.
Local reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.

[[jjdx_cli]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_command_group⟧
{jjdx_cli}::
The Job Jockey command group within
{jjdx_vvx}.
Invoked as `vvx jjx`.
All operations are subcommands of
{jjdx_cli}.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.

== Commit Message Architecture

All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations

== Operations

Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access (read, transform, atomic write).
Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
{jjda_file}.

=== Write Operations

[[jjdo_nominate]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_nominate}

Create a new
{jjdhr_heat}
with empty
{jjdpr_pace}
structure.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
* {jjda_file}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjda_silks}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjda_created}

{jjds_stdout} New
{jjdt_firemark}

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. If
{jjdgr_gallops}
file does not exist:
.. Create parent directory `.claude/jjm/` if needed
.. Initialize new
{jjdgr_gallops}
with
{jjdgm_seed}
`AA` and empty `heats`
. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file (or use newly created)
. Read current
{jjdgm_seed}
as
{jjdt_firemark}
. Compute paddock path: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the 2 base64 chars
. Create paddock file with template:
+
----
# Paddock: {silks}

## Context

(Describe the initiative's background and goals)

## References

(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)
----
. Create new
{jjdhr_heat}
with:
.. Key: `₣` + encoded
{jjdt_firemark}
.. {jjdhm_silks}:
from argument
.. {jjdhm_created}:
from argument
.. {jjdhm_status}:
{jjdhe_current}
.. {jjdhm_order}:
empty array
.. {jjdhm_seed}:
`AAA` (index 0)
.. {jjdhm_paddock}:
computed paddock path
.. `paces`: empty object
. Increment
{jjdgm_seed}
(base64 arithmetic with carry)
. Write transformed
{jjdgr_gallops}
to file (atomic)
. Output new
{jjdt_firemark}
to stdout

[[jjdo_slate]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_slate}

Add a new
{jjdpr_pace}
to a
{jjdhr_heat}.
The initial
{jjdkr_tack}
is created with state
{jjdpe_rough}.

By default, the new
{jjdpr_pace}
is appended to the end of

Use positioning flags to insert at a specific location.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
* {jjda_file}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) — target
{jjdhr_heat}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjda_silks}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* stdin —
{jjdkm_text}
content for the initial
{jjdkr_tack}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_before}
— insert before specified
{jjdt_coronet}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_after}
— insert after specified
{jjdt_coronet}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_first}
— insert at beginning of
{jjdhm_order}

*Positioning:* Only one of
{jjda_before},
{jjda_after},
or
{jjda_first}
may be specified.
If none provided, append to end (default).
Note:
{jjdo_rail}
reorders existing
{jjdpr_pace_s};
{jjdo_slate}
positioning inserts a new
{jjdpr_pace}
at a specific location.

{jjds_stdout} New
{jjdt_coronet}

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file
. Verify
{jjdhr_heat}
exists with given
{jjdt_firemark}
. If
{jjda_before}
or
{jjda_after}
provided, verify target
{jjdt_coronet}
exists in
{jjdhr_heat}
. Read
{jjdhm_seed}
from target
{jjdhr_heat}
. Construct
{jjdt_coronet}:
`₢` + encoded
{jjdt_firemark}
(2 chars) +
{jjdhm_seed}
(3 chars)
. Create new
{jjdpr_pace}
with:
.. Key: constructed
{jjdt_coronet}
.. `tacks`: array with single
{jjdkr_tack}:
... {jjdkm_refined}:
current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
... {jjdkm_state}:
{jjdpe_rough}
... {jjdkm_text}:
from stdin
... {jjdkm_silks}:
from argument
... {jjdkm_commit}:
capture {jjdkm_commit}
. Insert
{jjdt_coronet}

{jjda_first}:

{jjdt_coronet}
.. If
{jjda_after}:
insert immediately after target
{jjdt_coronet}
.. Otherwise: append to end

{jjdhm_seed}
(base64 arithmetic with carry)
. Write transformed

[[jjdo_rail]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_rail}

Reorder
{jjdpr_pace_s}
within a
{jjdhr_heat}
by updating
{jjdhm_order}.
Supports two modes: *order mode* replaces the entire sequence; *move mode* relocates a single
{jjdpr_pace}.

*Order mode* (full replacement):
----
vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> <CORONET>...
----

*Move mode* (single relocation):
----
vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> --move <CORONET> [--before <TARGET> | --after <TARGET> | --first | --last]
----

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
* {jjda_file}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) — target
{jjdhr_heat}

*Order mode arguments:*

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* order — array of
{jjdt_coronet_s}
in desired sequence (space-separated positional arguments)

*Move mode arguments:*

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjda_move}
— {jjdt_coronet}
to relocate

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* Exactly one positioning flag:
{jjda_before},
{jjda_after},
{jjda_first},
or
{jjda_last}

*Mode detection:* If
{jjda_move}
is present, move mode; otherwise order mode.

{jjds_stdout} Updated
{jjdhm_order}
array, one
{jjdt_coronet}
per line, in new sequence.

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

*Common steps:*

. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file
. Verify
{jjdhr_heat}
exists with given
{jjdt_firemark}

*Order mode:*

. Parse positional arguments into array of
{jjdt_coronet_s}
. Validate order array:
.. Count must match current
{jjdhm_order}
length
.. No duplicate
{jjdt_coronet_s}
.. All
{jjdt_coronet_s}
must exist in
{jjdhr_heat_p}
`paces` object
.. All
{jjdt_coronet_s}
must embed correct parent
{jjdt_firemark}
. Replace
{jjdhm_order}
with new order array
. Write transformed
{jjdgr_gallops}
to file (atomic)
. Output new
{jjdhm_order},
one
{jjdt_coronet}
per line

*Move mode:*

. Validate
{jjda_move}
{jjdt_coronet}
exists in
{jjdhr_heat}
. Validate exactly one positioning flag provided
. If
{jjda_before}
or
{jjda_after}:
validate target
{jjdt_coronet}
exists and differs from
{jjda_move}
{jjdt_coronet}
. Remove
{jjda_move}
{jjdt_coronet}
from current position in
{jjdhm_order}
. Insert at new position:
.. If
{jjda_first}:
prepend to beginning
.. If
{jjda_last}:
append to end
.. If
{jjda_before}:
insert immediately before target
{jjdt_coronet}
.. If
{jjda_after}:
insert immediately after target
{jjdt_coronet}
. Write transformed
{jjdgr_gallops}
to file (atomic)
. Output new
{jjdhm_order},
one
{jjdt_coronet}
per line

*Validation errors:*

[cols="2,3"]
|===
| Condition | Error

| {jjda_move} with positional coronets
| "Cannot combine --move with positional coronets"

| {jjda_move} {jjdt_coronet} not found
| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"

| No positioning flag provided
| "Move mode requires exactly one positioning flag"

| Multiple positioning flags
| "Conflicting positioning flags: {flags}"

| Target same as move
| "Cannot position pace relative to itself"

| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found
| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
|===

[[jjdo_draft]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_draft}

Move a
{jjdpr_pace}
from one
{jjdhr_heat}
to another.
This is a primitive operation: mechanical transfer with no ceremony.
The
{jjdpr_pace}
receives a new
{jjdt_coronet}
in the destination
{jjdhr_heat};
all
{jjdkr_tack}
history is preserved.

*Important:* "Drafted" is not a
{jjdpr_pace}
state.
{jjdpr_pace}
states remain:
{jjdpe_rough},
{jjdpe_bridled},
{jjdpe_complete},
{jjdpe_abandoned}.
Draft is an operation that moves
{jjdpr_pace_s}
between
{jjdhr_heat_s};
state is preserved through the move.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
* {jjda_file}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_coronet}
(positional) —
{jjdpr_pace}
to move

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjda_to}
— destination
{jjdt_firemark}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_before}
— insert before specified
{jjdt_coronet}
in destination

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_after}
— insert after specified
{jjdt_coronet}
in destination

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_first}
— insert at beginning of destination
{jjdhm_order}

*Positioning:* Only one of
{jjda_before},
{jjda_after},
or
{jjda_first}
may be specified.
If none provided, append to end of destination
{jjdhm_order}
(default).

{jjds_stdout} New
{jjdt_coronet}
in destination
{jjdhr_heat}

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file
. Extract source
{jjdt_firemark}
from
{jjdt_coronet}
(first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
. Verify source
{jjdhr_heat}
exists
. Verify
{jjdpr_pace}
exists in source
{jjdhr_heat}
. Verify destination
{jjdhr_heat}
exists with given
{jjdt_firemark}
. If
{jjda_before}
or
{jjda_after}

{jjdt_coronet}
exists in destination
{jjdhr_heat}

{jjdhm_seed}

{jjdt_coronet}:
`₢` + destination
{jjdt_firemark}
(2 chars) + destination
{jjdhm_seed}
(3 chars)

{jjdpr_pace}
data to destination with new
{jjdt_coronet}:
.. Prepend new
{jjdkr_tack}

current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
... {jjdkm_state}:
inherited from previous `tacks[0].state`
... {jjdkm_text}:
`Drafted from {old_coronet} in {source_firemark}.\n\n` + previous `tacks[0].text`
... {jjdkm_silks}:
inherited from previous `tacks[0].silks`
... {jjdkm_commit}:
capture {jjdkm_commit}
.. Preserve remaining
{jjdkr_tack}
history
. Insert new
{jjdt_coronet}
into destination
{jjdhm_order}
at determined position
. Increment destination
{jjdhm_seed}
. Remove
{jjdpr_pace}
from source
{jjdhr_heat}:
.. Remove from source `paces` object
.. Remove from source
{jjdhm_order}
. Write transformed
{jjdgr_gallops}
to file (atomic)
. Output new
{jjdt_coronet}
to stdout

*Validation errors:*

[cols="2,3"]
|===
| Condition | Error

| Source and destination same
| "Cannot draft pace to same heat"

| Source {jjdpr_pace} not found
| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"

| Destination {jjdhr_heat} not found
| "Heat {firemark} not found"

| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found in destination
| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
|===

Add a new

This is the unified operation for all

{jjdgr_gallops}

argument (required)

[[jjdo_retire]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_retire}

Archive a
{jjdhr_heat}
by writing a trophy file, removing the heat from
{jjdgr_gallops},
deleting the paddock file, and committing.
This is a destructive operation that permanently removes the heat from active tracking.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
* {jjda_file}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) — target
{jjdhr_heat}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* `--execute` — perform the retire operation.
Without this flag, outputs trophy markdown to stdout as a dry-run preview.

{jjds_stdout}
Without `--execute`: trophy markdown content (dry-run preview).
With `--execute`: path to the written trophy file.

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

Without `--execute` (dry-run):

. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file
. Verify
{jjdhr_heat}
exists with given
{jjdt_firemark}
. Read paddock file contents from
{jjdhm_paddock}
. Call
{jjdo_rein}
internally to get steeplechase entries
. Build trophy markdown (see format below)
. Output trophy markdown to stdout

With `--execute`:

. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file
. Verify
{jjdhr_heat}
exists with given
{jjdt_firemark}
. Extract
{jjdhr_heat}
metadata:
{jjdhm_silks},
{jjdhm_created}
. Read paddock file contents from
{jjdhm_paddock}
. Call
{jjdo_rein}
internally to get steeplechase entries
. Build trophy markdown:
+
----
# Heat Trophy: {silks}

**Firemark:** {firemark}
**Created:** {created}
**Retired:** {today}
**Status:** retired

## Paddock

{full paddock content}

## Paces

{for each pace in order:}

### {pace_silks} ({coronet}) [{final_state}]

{for each tack, newest first:}
**[{ts}] {state}**

{tack text}

{if tack has direction:}
*Direction:* {direction}

## Steeplechase

{for each steeplechase entry from rein, newest first:}

### {date} - {pace_silks or "Heat"} - {marker or "commit"}

{subject/description}

----
. Compute trophy filename: `.claude/jjm/retired/jjh_b{created}-r{today}-{silks}.md`
where `{today}` is current date in YYMMDD format
. Create `.claude/jjm/retired/` directory if it does not exist
. Write trophy markdown to trophy file
. Remove
{jjdhr_heat}
entry from
{jjdgr_gallops}
`heats` object
. Write transformed
{jjdgr_gallops}
to file (atomic)
. Delete paddock file at
{jjdhm_paddock}
. Stage exactly three paths: trophy file, gallops.json, paddock file (as deletion)
. Run size guard with 200KB limit (trophy files can be large for big heats)
. Commit with message per steeplechase conventions (format TBD by unify-commit-format)
. Release lock
. Output trophy file path to stdout

*Format Modes*

| Mode | Purpose | Output

| `overview`

| One line per pace: `[state] silks (₢coronet)`

| `order`
| Dependency check

| `detail`
| Inspect one pace
| Full tack text for specified pace (requires `--pace`)

| `full`
| Planning context
| Paddock content + all paces with tack text
|===

{jjds_stdout} Human-readable text (not JSON).

*Example output — overview:*

----

. Read

from file

. Match on format mode:
.. *overview*: For each pace in
{jjdhm_order},
print `[state] silks (coronet)` where silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`
.. *order*: Same as overview with line numbers
.. *detail*: Find specified pace, print header and full tack text
.. *full*: Print heat header, paddock content, then each pace with tack text

=== Steeplechase Operations

The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
{jjdgr_gallops}
JSON.
Commit messages follow patterns that
{jjdo_rein}
can parse to reconstruct session history.

==== Commit Message Patterns

All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:IDENTITY[:ACTION]: message`

* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
* `BRAND` — repository identifier baked into arcanum at install time (e.g., "RBM")
* `HALLMARK` — Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)

*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
----
jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:n: commit message

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.

*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
----
jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:A: description

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

Pace-level action codes:

* `n` — Notch: standard commit
* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)

*Heat-level operations*:
----
jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:N: heat-silks
jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:S: pace-silks
jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:r: reordered
jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:T: pace-silks
jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
jjb:BRAND:HALLMARK:₣XX:R: heat-silks
----

Heat-level action codes:

* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
* `S` — Slate: add new pace
* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)

[[jjdo_rein]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_rein}

Parse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
{jjdhr_heat}.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) — target
{jjdhr_heat}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* `--limit` — maximum entries to return (default: 50)

{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
[source,json]
----
[
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "commit": "abc123ef",
    "hallmark": "1009",
    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
    "action": "n",
    "subject": "commit message"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "commit": "def456ab",
    "hallmark": "1009-abc1234",
    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
    "action": "A",
    "subject": "description"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "hallmark": "1009",
    "action": "N",
    "subject": "heat-silks"
  }
]
----
Field `hallmark` is present for new-format commits, absent for old-format commits.
Field `coronet` is present for pace-level entries, absent for heat-level operations.
Field `action` is present for all commits, including standard notch commits (`n`).
Entries ordered newest first.

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:[^:]+:(₣XX|₢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  (matches any brand; filtering is by identity, not brand)
. Parse each line: extract timestamp, identity, action (if present), and message
. For pace-level (₢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
. For heat-level (₣FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
. Output JSON

[[jjdo_notch]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_notch}

Create a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_coronet}
(positional) — active pace identity (embeds parent heat)

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* `--size-limit` — size limit in bytes for the commit guard (default: 50KB).
Use when the pace spec justifies legitimate large commits.

{jjds_stdout} Commit hash

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
. Stage changes (`git add -u`)
. Run size guard check (uses `--size-limit` if provided, otherwise 50KB default)
. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
. Format full message: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
. Run `git commit -m "..."`
. Release lock
. Output commit hash

[[jjdo_chalk]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_chalk}

Create an empty commit marking a steeplechase event.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* identity (positional) — {jjdt_coronet} for pace-level, {jjdt_firemark} for heat-level discussion

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* `--marker` — marker type: `A`, `W`, `F`, or `d` (single letter codes)

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* `--description` — marker description text

{jjds_stdout} Commit hash

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
. Detect identity type by length (5 chars = coronet, 2 chars = firemark)
. Validate: `A`/`W`/`F` require coronet; `d` accepts either
. Format message:
.. If coronet: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:X: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
.. If firemark (discussion only): `jjb:BRAND:₣XX:d: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
. Run `git commit --allow-empty -m "..."`
. Release lock
. Output commit hash
